description.task: Implement constructor injection in Java. Build a ReportService that requires both a DataSource and an EmailSender injected through its constructor.
instructions: Add a constructor to ReportService that takes DataSource and EmailSender as parameters and assigns them to final fields.
generateAndEmailReport() fetches data from dataSource and sends it via emailSender.
In Main, create PostgresDataSource and SmtpEmailSender, inject them into ReportService, then call generateAndEmailReport().
description.hint: Constructor injection is the preferred Spring DI method — it makes dependencies explicit and supports immutability (final fields).

description.task: Implement setter injection in Java. Build a NotificationManager that allows optional dependencies to be set via setters after construction.
instructions: Add setLogger(Logger logger) method to NotificationManager.
sendNotification() calls logger.log() only if logger is not null, then prints '[serviceName] Notification: [message]'.
In Main, first create NotificationManager without logger and send a notification.
Then set ConsoleLogger and send another notification.
description.hint: Setter injection is useful for optional dependencies. Spring uses @Autowired on setter methods for this pattern.
description.expectedOutput:
AlertService Notification: Server started
[LOG] Sending notification: Disk usage critical
AlertService Notification: Disk usage critical

description.task: Implement a simple field injection mechanism using Java Reflection. Create a custom @Inject annotation and a minimal injector that sets annotated fields.
description.context: Spring's @Autowired field injection uses reflection internally. Understanding this helps you understand how Spring works under the hood.
instructions: In inject(), iterate all declared fields, check for @Inject annotation, look up the field type in registry, and set the value using field.setAccessible(true).
In Main, create Injector, register ProductRepository, create ProductService, inject, then call display(5).
Output should show the found product.
description.hint: Use field.setAccessible(true) to access private fields via reflection. Then field.set(target, value) to inject the dependency.
